hero.subtitle: Unique handcrafted ceramics. Every piece tells its own story.
hero.heroImageAlt: Alicja drinking from a Kraken mug by the sea

home.introText: Every piece is unique — hand-thrown, carefully glazed, and fired at high temperatures. We don't mass-produce. We create to order.
home.interestedText: Write to us — we'd love to create something special just for you.

collection.heading: Collection
collection.subtitle: Every piece is unique — hand-thrown and carefully glazed with attention to detail.
collection.interestedText: Write to us — we'd love to create something special just for you.

contactModal.description: Every piece is made to order. Write to us and we'll create something special just for you.

about.heading: About Delulu Ceramics
about.subtitle: Discover our story
about.storyTitle: From a van to a ceramics studio...
about.storyP1: My ceramics were born on the road. Most of the designs you see here have their roots in a van trip through sunny Spain. It was there, between one stop and the next, that I soaked in the colours, flavours, and scents that I now capture in clay.
about.storyP2: I'm inspired by everything that evokes freedom: the deep blue of the sea, the taste of shared meals, and fascinating underwater creatures. That's why my work isn't perfectly straight — it carries the energy of nature. An example? My blue tapas platter, which looks more like an underwater rock than a dish.
about.storyP3: I create for those who, like me, love to celebrate moments and want a piece of sunshine at home, regardless of the weather outside.
about.step1Text: Every vessel is hand-thrown on a potter's wheel from natural clay.
about.step2Text: Pieces are coated with hand-mixed glazes in unique colours.
about.step3Text: Double firing in a ceramic kiln gives durability and a beautiful finish.

contact.heading: Contact
contact.subtitle: Want to order ceramics or have questions?
contact.description: Get in touch with Alicja through your preferred channel. We're happy to answer questions, give advice, and take orders.

kubek-kraken.name: Kraken Mug
kubek-kraken.description: A hand-thrown mug with sculpted kraken tentacles wrapping around the vessel. Each piece is unique — glazed in deep ocean tones, from violet to dark blue. Tentacles are formed by hand, one by one.

images.0: Kraken Mug — close-up of tentacles and glaze
images.1: Kraken Mug — side view against snow
images.2: Kraken Mug — glaze and tentacle details
images.3: Kraken Mug — close-up of suction cups
images.4: Kraken Mug — on a stone by the sea

kubki-piaskowe.name: Sandy Mugs
kubki-piaskowe.description: Mugs in warm, sandy tones with a blue interior — like a beach meeting the ocean. Speckled texture on the outside, deep blue inside. Comfortable handle and perfect size for your morning coffee.

images.0: Sandy Mug — interior view with blue glaze
images.1: Sandy Mugs — pair of mugs in hand
images.2: Sandy Mugs — top view of blue interiors

czarki.name: Tea Bowls
czarki.description: A collection of small handleless vessels — tea bowls, heart-shaped cups, and ceramic to-go cups. Multicoloured glazes, romantic shapes, and ceramic versions of travel mugs. Wabi-sabi style, where imperfection is beauty.

images.0: Tea Bowls — pair of bowls with blue interior
images.1: Tea Bowls — side view of multicoloured glaze

miski-oceaniczne.name: Ocean Bowls
miski-oceaniczne.description: Hand-thrown bowls inspired by the depths of the ocean. Glazed in shades of blue and turquoise, with organic shapes reminiscent of sea rocks. Perfect for serving salads or your favourite soup.

images.0: Ocean Bowls — set of bowls in shades of blue
images.1: Ocean Bowls — glaze details and texture

miski-piaskowe.name: Sandy Bowls
miski-piaskowe.description: Bowls in sandy tones with abstract, brushstroke patterns in dark glaze. Every swirl is different — painted by hand in a single brushstroke. A set of three bowls in different sizes.

images.0: Sandy Bowls — three bowls from above with brushstroke patterns
images.2: Sandy Bowls — stack of bowls in profile
images.3: Sandy Bowls — spread out on fabric

patera-rafa.name: Reef Platter
patera-rafa.description: A large platter with organic, undulating edges — inspired by coral reefs. Glazed in deep blue with a detail resembling coral. Every piece is one of a kind, perfect as a table centrepiece.

images.0: Reef Platter — close-up of coral detail and blue glaze
images.1: Reef Platter — edge detail with coral
images.2: Reef Platter — two platters on a wooden table
images.3: Reef Platter — side view of undulating edge

naczynie-na-tapas.name: Tapas Dish
naczynie-na-tapas.description: A tapas serving set in a raw, sandy finish with a distinctive navy splatter. A bowl with a small platter — together they make the perfect set for shared meals in Spanish style.

images.0: Tapas Dish — bowl and platter from above
